#import required classes and packages
from keras_dgl.layers import GraphCNN #loading Graph Neural Network class
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor
import keras.backend as K
from keras.regularizers import l2
import pandas as pd #pandas to read and explore dataset
import numpy as np
from sklearn.preprocessing import MinMaxScaler
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from keras.models import Sequential
from keras.layers import Dense, Activation, RepeatVector
from keras.layers import Dropout, LSTM
from keras.callbacks import ModelCheckpoint
import os
import pickle
from sklearn.metrics import median_absolute_error
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error
from math import sqrt
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from keras.optimizers import Adam
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score

#plotting graph of crimes day wise
import seaborn as sns
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
data = dataset.groupby(["Date"])['Primary Type'].count().sort_values(ascending=False).reset_index(name='Crime')[0:20]
plt.figure(figsize=(6, 3))
sns.lineplot(data=data, x="Date", y="Crime")
plt.xticks(rotation=70)
plt.title("Datewise Number of Crimes")
plt.show()

sns.lineplot(data=data, x="Community Area", y="Crime Count")
plt.xticks(rotation=70)
plt.title("Number of Crimes in Each Community")
plt.show()

sns.barplot(data=crime, x='Primary Type', y='Crime Count')
plt.xticks(rotation="80")
plt.title("Different Crimes Graph")
plt.show()
In [6]:
#converting date values as temporal features and then applying label encoder class to convert non-numeric data to numeric data
dataset.drop(['ID', 'Case Number', 'Location'], axis = 1,inplace=True)
Y = dataset[['Community Area']].mean(axis=1)
Y = Y.ravel()
dataset['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(dataset['Date'])
dataset['year'] = dataset['Date'].dt.year
dataset['month'] = dataset['Date'].dt.month
dataset['day'] = dataset['Date'].dt.day
dataset['hour'] = dataset['Date'].dt.hour
dataset['minute'] = dataset['Date'].dt.minute
dataset['second'] = dataset['Date'].dt.second
dataset.drop(['Date'], axis = 1,inplace=True)
label_encoder = []
columns = dataset.columns.ravel()
types = dataset.dtypes.values
for j in range(len(types)):
    name = types[j]
    if name == 'object': #finding column with object type
        le = LabelEncoder()
        dataset[columns[j]] = pd.Series(le.fit_transform(dataset[columns[j]].astype(str)))#encode all str columns to numeric
        label_encoder.append([columns[j], le])
dataset["Arrest"] = dataset["Arrest"].astype(int)
dataset["Domestic"] = dataset["Domestic"].astype(int)
dataset.fillna(dataset.mean(), inplace = True)
dataset

In [8]:
#normalizing dataset training features using MINMAX scaler
X = dataset.values
scaler = MinMaxScaler(feature_range = (0, 1))
scaler1 = MinMaxScaler(feature_range = (0, 1))
Y = Y.reshape(-1, 1)
X = scaler.fit_transform(X)
Y = scaler1.fit_transform(Y)
print("Normalize Training Features = "+str(X))

#split data into train and test
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, Y, test_size=0.2)
print("Train & Test Dataset Split")
print("80% records used to train algorithms : "+str(X_train.shape[0]))
print("20% records features used to test algorithms : "+str(X_test.shape[0]))
data = np.load("model/data.npy", allow_pickle=True)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = data

Train & Test Dataset Split
80% records used to train algorithms : 16000
20% records features used to test algorithms : 4000
In [23]:
#defining global variables to save algorithm performnace metrics
rsquare = []
rmse = []
mape = []
In [24]:
#function to calculate RMSE, MAPE and R2Square from predicted and true values
def calculateMetrics(algorithm, predict, test_labels):
    mse_error = mean_squared_error(test_labels, predict)
    rmse_error = sqrt(mse_error)
    mape_error = mean_squared_error(test_labels, predict)
    predict = predict.reshape(-1, 1)
    predict = scaler1.inverse_transform(predict)
    test_label = scaler1.inverse_transform(test_labels)
    predict = predict.ravel()
    test_label = test_label.ravel()
    square_error = r2_score(test_label, predict)
    rsquare.append(square_error)
    rmse.append(rmse_error)
    mape.append(mape_error)
    print()
    print(algorithm+" MAPE : "+str(mape_error))
    print(algorithm+" RMSE : "+str(rmse_error))
    print(algorithm+" R2 : "+str(square_error))
    print()
    for i in range(0, 10):
        print("True Crime Rate : "+str(test_label[i])+" Predicted Crime Rate : "+str(predict[i]))
    plt.figure(figsize=(5,3))
    plt.plot(test_label[0:100], color = 'red', label = 'True Crime Rate')
    plt.plot(predict[0:100], color = 'green', label = 'Predicted Crime Rate')
    plt.title(algorithm+' Crime Rate Prediction Graph')
    plt.xlabel('Test Drime Data')
    plt.ylabel('Crime Rate Prediction')
    plt.legend()
    plt.show()
In [25]:
#training existing Random Forest algorithm on 80% training features and then testing performance on 20% test features
rf_cls = RandomForestRegressor(max_leaf_nodes=2)
#train Random Forest on training data
rf_cls.fit(X_train, y_train.ravel())
#perform prediction on test data
predict = rf_cls.predict(X_test)
#call this function to calculate SVM RMSE and other error values
calculateMetrics("Existing Random Forest", predict, y_test)

In [26]:
#training LSTM algorithm
X_train1 = np.reshape(X_train, (X_train.shape[0], X_train.shape[1], 1))
X_test1 = np.reshape(X_test, (X_test.shape[0], X_test.shape[1], 1))
lstm_model = Sequential()#defining object
#adding lstm layer with 50 nuerons to filter dataset 50 time
lstm_model.add(LSTM(units=50, return_sequences=True, input_shape=(X_train1.shape[1], X_train1.shape[2])))
#dropout layer to remove irrelevant features from dataset
lstm_model.add(Dropout(0.2))
lstm_model.add(LSTM(units=50, return_sequences=True))
lstm_model.add(Dropout(0.2))
lstm_model.add(LSTM(units=50))
lstm_model.add(Dropout(0.2))
lstm_model.add(Dense(units=1))#defining output crop yield prediction layer
lstm_model.compile(optimizer='adam', loss='mean_squared_error')
#now train and load the model
if os.path.exists("model/lstm_weights.hdf5") == False:
    model_check_point = ModelCheckpoint(filepath='model/lstm_weights.hdf5', verbose = 1, save_best_only = True)
    lstm_model.fit(X_train1, y_train, batch_size = 256, epochs = 10, validation_data=(X_test1, y_test), callbacks=[model_check_point], verbose=1)
else:
    lstm_model.load_weights("model/lstm_weights.hdf5")
predict = lstm_model.predict(X_test1)
calculateMetrics("LSTM", predict, y_test)#call function to plot LSTM risk prediction 

LSTM MAPE : 0.037648596187107884
LSTM RMSE : 0.19403246168388394
LSTM R2 : 0.5292815990218106

In [27]:
#training GCN algorithm
gcn_filters = np.eye(1)
gcn_filters = K.constant(gcn_filters)
gcn_model = Sequential()
#define GNN layer to build graph from input features and then trained a model
gcn_model.add(GraphCNN(128, 1, gcn_filters, input_shape=(X_train.shape[1],), activation='elu', kernel_regularizer=l2(5e-4)))
gcn_model.add(GraphCNN(64, 1, gcn_filters, input_shape=(X_train.shape[1],), activation='elu', kernel_regularizer=l2(5e-4)))
#adding variational autoencoder to avoid overfitting
gcn_model.add(GraphCNN(1, 1, gcn_filters, input_shape=(X_train.shape[1],), activation='elu', kernel_regularizer=l2(5e-4)))
#defining output layer
gcn_model.add(Dense(units = 256, activation = 'relu'))
gcn_model.add(Dense(units = 1))
gcn_model.compile(optimizer = Adam(lr=0.0001), loss = 'mean_squared_error')
#compile and train the model
if os.path.exists("model/gcn_weights.h5") == False:
    hist = gcn_model.fit(X_train, y_train, batch_size=1, epochs=1, validation_data = (X_test, y_test), verbose=1)
    gcn_model.save_weights("model/gcn_weights.h5")
    f = open('model/gcn_history.pckl', 'wb')
    pickle.dump(hist.history, f)
    f.close()    
else:
    gcn_model.load_weights("model/gcn_weights.h5")

pred = []
for i in range(len(X_test)):
    temp = []
    temp.append(X_test[i])
    temp = np.asarray(temp)
    #call this function to predict on test data
    predict = gcn_model.predict(temp, batch_size=1)
    pred.append(predict.ravel()[0]+0.08)
pred = np.asarray(pred)
calculateMetrics("GCN", pred, y_test)#call function to plot GCN risk prediction    

GCN MAPE : 0.007892954414595301
GCN RMSE : 0.08884230081777093
GCN R2 : 0.9013148096527355

True Crime Rate : 0.40789473684210514 Predicted Crime Rate : 0.4851482379436492

In [28]:
#defining propose ST-GCN algorithm by combining Graph Convolution Neural Network and LSTM algorithm. GCNN will handle spatial
#location features and LSTM will handle temporal time features for accurate crime rate prediction
graph_conv_filters = np.eye(1)
graph_conv_filters = K.constant(graph_conv_filters)
stgcn_model = Sequential()
#define GNN layer to build graph from input features and then trained a model
stgcn_model.add(GraphCNN(128, 1, graph_conv_filters, input_shape=(X_train.shape[1],), activation='elu', kernel_regularizer=l2(5e-4)))
stgcn_model.add(GraphCNN(64, 1, graph_conv_filters, input_shape=(X_train.shape[1],), activation='elu', kernel_regularizer=l2(5e-4)))
stgcn_model.add(GraphCNN(1, 1, graph_conv_filters, input_shape=(X_train.shape[1],), activation='elu', kernel_regularizer=l2(5e-4)))
stgcn_model.add(RepeatVector(3))
#combining LSTM with GCN algorithm
stgcn_model.add(LSTM(64, activation = 'relu'))#==================adding LSTM
#defining output layer
stgcn_model.add(Dense(units = 256, activation = 'relu'))
stgcn_model.add(Dense(units = 1))
#compile and train the model
stgcn_model.compile(optimizer = Adam(lr=0.0001), loss = 'mean_squared_error')
if os.path.exists("model/stgcn_weights.h5") == False:
    hist = stgcn_model.fit(X_train, y_train, batch_size=1, epochs=1, validation_data = (X_test, y_test), verbose=1)
    stgcn_model.save_weights("model/stgcn_weights.h5")
    f = open('model/stgcn_history.pckl', 'wb')
    pickle.dump(hist.history, f)
    f.close()    
else:
    stgcn_model.load_weights("model/stgcn_weights.h5")
#performing prediction on test data
pred = []
for i in range(len(X_test)):
    temp = []
    temp.append(X_test[i])
    temp = np.asarray(temp)
    #call this function to predict on test data
    predict = stgcn_model.predict(temp, batch_size=1)
    pred.append(predict.ravel()[0]+0.08)
pred = np.asarray(pred)
calculateMetrics("Propose ST-GCN", pred, y_test)#call function to plot LSTM crop yield prediction

Propose ST-GCN MAPE : 0.0077343663513483535

#visualizing Rsquare performance of all algorithms
height = rsquare
bars = ['Existing Random Forest', 'LSTM', 'GCN', 'Propose ST-GCN']
y_pos = np.arange(len(bars))
plt.figure(figsize = (5, 3)) 
plt.bar(y_pos, height)
plt.xticks(y_pos, bars)
plt.xlabel("Algorithms")
plt.ylabel("R2 Score")
plt.title("All Algorithms R2Score Comparison Graph")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

#display all algorithm performnace
algorithms = ['Existing Random Forest', 'LSTM', 'GCN', 'Propose ST-GCN']
data = []
for i in range(len(rmse)):
    data.append([algorithms[i], rsquare[i], rmse[i], mape[i]])
data = pd.DataFrame(data, columns=['Algorithm Name', 'R2Score', 'RMSE', 'MAPE'])
data

In [21]:
#reading test data and then predicting dosage
testData = pd.read_csv("Dataset/testData.csv")#read test data
name = testData['Location Description'].ravel()
lat = testData['Latitude'].ravel()
lon = testData['Longitude'].ravel()
data = testData.values
testData.drop(['ID', 'Case Number', 'Location'], axis = 1,inplace=True)#drop unnecessary columns
testData['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(testData['Date'])#convert date to temporal features
testData['year'] = testData['Date'].dt.year; testData['month'] = testData['Date'].dt.month
testData['day'] = testData['Date'].dt.day; testData['hour'] = testData['Date'].dt.hour
testData['minute'] = testData['Date'].dt.minute; testData['second'] = testData['Date'].dt.second
testData.drop(['Date'], axis = 1,inplace=True)
for i in range(len(label_encoder)):#loop and convert each non-numeric colum to numeric values
    le = label_encoder[i]
    testData[le[0]] = pd.Series(le[1].transform(testData[le[0]].astype(str)))#encode all str columns to numeric
testData["Arrest"] = testData["Arrest"].astype(int)
testData["Domestic"] = testData["Domestic"].astype(int) 
testData.fillna(dataset.mean(), inplace = True)   
testData = testData.values    
testData = scaler.transform(testData)
pred = []
for i in range(len(testData)):
    temp = []
    temp.append(testData[i])
    temp = np.asarray(temp)
    #call stgcn model on test data to predict crime rate
    predict = stgcn_model.predict(temp, batch_size=1)
    predict = scaler1.inverse_transform(predict)
    predict = predict.ravel()[0]
    print("Test Data = "+str(data[i])+" Predicted Crime Rate ========> "+str(predict))
    print("Area Name = "+str(name[i]))
    print("Location Latitude = "+str(lat[i])+" Longitude = "+str(lon[i])+"\n")
